context: Logs smoke test – planning only, no code implementation in this iteration.

items.title: Review and refine logs smoke test requirements
items.description: Confirm that the goals, scope, and constraints in data/specification.md match the actual project needs and logging architecture.

items.title: Define sample log events for smoke testing
items.description: Design a small, deterministic set of log entries (services, levels, timestamps) to be used in the smoke test.

items.title: Decide how the smoke test will be invoked
items.description: Select or define a future CLI or script entry point (e.g., `pnpm test:logs:smoke` or similar) without implementing it yet.

items.title: Define basic observability and success criteria
items.description: Specify what signals (file existence, line counts, markers) will be checked to decide if the smoke test passed.

items.title: Decide on log artifact cleanup strategy
items.description: Determine whether the smoke test should clean up logs automatically, keep them for inspection, or support a flag to control cleanup.
